data.title: Assessing Boliden (OM:BOL) Valuation Ahead Of Its Q4 2025 Interim Report Release
data.description:
Boliden (OM:BOL) is in focus ahead of its Interim Report for Q4 2025, due on 3 February, as investors look toward fresh financial data and commentary from CEO Mikael Staffas and CFO Håkan Gabrielsson.

See our latest analysis for Boliden.

The upcoming Q4 update comes after a period of strong momentum, with a 30 day share price return of 23.5% and a 90 day gain of 46.22%, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 73.94% reflects how the stock has rewarded investors over a longer stretch.

If Boliden’s recent strength has you reassessing the wider sector, this could be a good moment to scan other aerospace and defense stocks that might fit your watchlist next.

With the share price well above the average analyst target, yet an intrinsic value model implying a 43.21% discount, the big question is whether Boliden still offers a buying opportunity or if the market is already pricing in future growth.

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 37.4% Overvalued

Compared with Boliden’s last close at SEK595.40, the most followed narrative points to a fair value of SEK433.47, using a 6.52% discount rate and detailed long term assumptions.

The analysts have a consensus price target of SEK310.067 for Boliden based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors. However, there is a degree of disagreement amongst analysts, with the most bullish reporting a price target of SEK350.0, and the most bearish reporting a price target of just SEK250.0.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how projected revenue growth, higher margins and a future P/E below today’s industry level combine to support that value gap? The full narrative lays out the step by step earnings path, the assumed profit mix across assets and the valuation multiple needed to make the numbers line up.

Result: Fair Value of SEK433.47 (OVERVALUED)

However, higher debt, combined with pressure points such as weaker zinc and nickel prices or operational setbacks at key mines, could quickly challenge these upbeat fair value assumptions.

While the popular narrative flags Boliden as 37.4% overvalued at SEK595.40 versus a fair value of SEK433.47, our DCF model lands in a very different place. Based on those cash flow assumptions, fair value is SEK1,048.51, which indicates a 43.2% discount at the current price. Same company, two very different perspectives. data.description:

See our latest analysis for Valmont Industries.

The recent 2.8% 1 day share price return and 7.2% 30 day share price return at US$440.03 build on a 32.0% 1 year total shareholder return, suggesting momentum has been gradually gaining strength.

If Valmont’s move has you thinking about where else capital is flowing, it could be a good moment to broaden your search with aerospace and defense stocks.

With shares near US$440 and analyst targets not far above that, plus only a modest intrinsic value gap, you have to ask yourself: is Valmont still undervalued, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 4.1% Undervalued

With Valmont’s last close at US$440.03 and the most followed narrative pointing to fair value of US$459, the current setup frames a small valuation gap that hinges on how its long term earnings story plays out.

Infrastructure investment and the accelerating energy transition are driving unprecedented demand in utility and transmission, supported by record customer backlogs and industry wide capacity constraints; Valmont's advanced investments in capacity, automation, and AI are expected to unlock $350 to $400 million in incremental annual revenue and support higher earnings and margins as this multi year cycle unfolds.

data.title: Should Weakness in Fortis Healthcare Limited's (NSE:FORTIS) Stock Be Seen As A Sign That Market Will Correct The Share Price Given Decent Financials?
data.description:
It is hard to get excited after looking at Fortis Healthcare's (NSE:FORTIS) recent performance, when its stock has declined 16% over the past three months. But if you pay close attention, you might find that its key financial indicators look quite decent, which could mean that the stock could potentially rise in the long-term given how markets usually reward more resilient long-term fundamentals. In this article, we decided to focus on Fortis Healthcare's ROE.

Return on equity or ROE is a key measure used to assess how efficiently a company's management is utilizing the company's capital. Simply put, it is used to assess the profitability of a company in relation to its equity capital.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

How To Calculate Return On Equity?

The formula for return on equity is:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Fortis Healthcare is:

11% = ₹10b ÷ ₹97b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the amount earned after tax over the last twelve months. So, this means that for every ₹1 of its shareholder's investments, the company generates a profit of ₹0.11.

Check out our latest analysis for Fortis Healthcare

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

Thus far, we have learned that ROE measures how efficiently a company is generating its profits. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Assuming all else is equal, companies that have both a higher return on equity and higher profit retention are usually the ones that have a higher growth rate when compared to companies that don't have the same features.

A Side By Side comparison of Fortis Healthcare's Earnings Growth And 11% ROE

When you first look at it, Fortis Healthcare's ROE doesn't look that attractive. However, given that the company's ROE is similar to the average industry ROE of 11%, we may spare it some thought. Moreover, we are quite pleased to see that Fortis Healthcare's net income grew significantly at a rate of 34% over the last five years. Taking into consideration that the ROE is not particularly high, we reckon that there could also be other factors at play which could be influencing the company's growth. Such as - high earnings retention or an efficient management in place.

We then compared Fortis Healthcare's net income growth with the industry and we're pleased to see that the company's growth figure is higher when compared with the industry which has a growth rate of 25% in the same 5-year period.

Earnings growth is a huge factor in stock valuation. It’s important for an investor to know whether the market has priced in the company's expected earnings growth (or decline). By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. If you're wondering about Fortis Healthcare's's valuation, check out this gauge of its price-to-earnings ratio, as compared to its industry.

Is Fortis Healthcare Efficiently Re-investing Its Profits?

Fortis Healthcare has a really low three-year median payout ratio of 13%, meaning that it has the remaining 87% left over to reinvest into its business. So it seems like the management is reinvesting profits heavily to grow its business and this reflects in its earnings growth number.

Besides, Fortis Healthcare has been paying dividends over a period of three years. This shows that the company is committed to sharing profits with its shareholders. Our latest analyst data shows that the future payout ratio of the company is expected to drop to 7.5% over the next three years. The fact that the company's ROE is expected to rise to 14% over the same period is explained by the drop in the payout ratio.

Conclusion

In total, it does look like Fortis Healthcare has some positive aspects to its business. Despite its low rate of return, the fact that the company reinvests a very high portion of its profits into its business, no doubt contributed to its high earnings growth. That being so, a study of the latest analyst forecasts show that the company is expected to see a slowdown in its future earnings growth. Are these analysts expectations based on the broad expectations for the industry, or on the company's fundamentals? Click here to be taken to our analyst's forecasts page for the company.

data.title: How Record NAV and KBK Exit Gains Will Impact Main Street Capital (MAIN) Investors
data.description:

data.title: A Look At ePlus (PLUS) Valuation After Mike Portegello Joins The Board
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Mike Portegello’s Appointment Matters for ePlus (PLUS)

ePlus (PLUS) has brought Mike Portegello onto its Board of Directors and key committees, a move that puts long experience in accounting, finance, and capital markets closer to the company’s decision making.

For you as an investor, this kind of board appointment is less about headlines and more about what it could mean for financial oversight, risk controls, and how the company evaluates growth and capital allocation decisions over time.

See our latest analysis for ePlus.

ePlus shares now trade at US$88.75, with a 1-day share price return of 2.31% and a 7-day share price return of 3.33%. The 90-day share price return of 24.33% contrasts with a 30-day share price return decline of 4.06%. The 1-year total shareholder return of 11.45% sits alongside a 3-year total shareholder return of 81% and a 5-year total shareholder return of 93.76%, suggesting longer term momentum has been stronger than in the most recent month, against a backdrop of fresh board appointments and recent director selling.

data.title: Is WeRide’s (WRD) WeChat Robotaxi Launch a Turning Point for Its Ecosystem Positioning?
data.description:
• WeRide has launched its "WeRide Go" Robotaxi Mini Program on Tencent’s WeChat, enabling users in Guangzhou’s Huangpu and Beijing’s Yizhuang districts to book autonomous rides directly within the super-app.
  • By embedding Robotaxi booking into a platform used by over 1 billion people, WeRide meaningfully lowers the friction to trial its autonomous driving service and embeds itself more deeply into China’s everyday mobility ecosystem.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this integration into WeChat’s massive ecosystem shapes WeRide’s investment narrative around user adoption and ecosystem positioning.

What Is WeRide's Investment Narrative?

For WeRide, the core belief as a shareholder is that its autonomous driving stack can translate into scaled, commercially relevant fleets across Robotaxi, Robobus and logistics, despite ongoing losses and a rich valuation. The new “WeRide Go” Mini Program on WeChat fits directly into the near term catalyst story around user adoption: it should reduce friction for first time riders and may support higher utilization in Guangzhou and Beijing, although the recent 10% share price drop suggests the market has not treated this as a game changer yet. The bigger swing factors still look like execution in overseas deployments, progress toward monetizing permits in the Middle East and China, and disciplined cash use, set against risks from persistent unprofitability, high price to sales multiples, governance questions and regulatory or geopolitical shocks that could slow deployment.

However, one key operational and financial risk may matter more than the WeChat boost for shareholders. Our valuation report unveils the possibility WeRide's shares may be trading at a premium.

data.title: Is Banc of California (BANC) Priced Attractive After Strong 1-Year Share Price Performance?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Banc of California at around US$20.87 is offering fair value right now, you are not alone.
  • The stock has logged returns of 2.0% over the last 7 days, 6.2% over the past month, 7.3% year to date and 35.0% over the last year, which naturally raises questions about how much of the story is already in the price.
  • Recent news around the stock has focused on how investors are reassessing US regional banks and looking more closely at individual balance sheets, capital levels and funding profiles. For Banc of California, that context helps explain why the share price and sentiment can shift quickly as new information and commentary emerge.
  • Banc of California currently holds a valuation score of 2 out of 6, so we will walk through what different valuation approaches say about the stock, and then finish with a framework that can help you judge whether that score really fits your own view of value.

Banc of California scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Banc of California Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a bank can earn on its equity above the return that shareholders require, then capitalizes those excess profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For Banc of California, the model starts with Book Value of US$19.10 per share and a Stable EPS estimate of US$1.98 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 9 analysts. The implied Cost of Equity is US$1.71 per share, which leaves an Excess Return of US$0.27 per share. That is supported by an Average Return on Equity of 8.94% and a Stable Book Value assumption of US$22.15 per share, sourced from weighted future Book Value estimates from 8 analysts.

Using these inputs, the Excess Returns model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about US$28.15 per share. Compared with the recent share price of around US$20.87, this indicates the stock is 25.9% undervalued under this framework.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests Banc of California is undervalued by 25.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 869 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Banc of California.

Approach 2: Banc of California Price vs Earnings

For a profitable bank, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings, especially when you want a quick cross check against other lenders and the broader Banks industry.

What counts as a "normal" P/E for a stock comes down to what the market expects for its future growth and how risky those earnings are perceived to be. Higher expected growth or lower risk can support a higher multiple, while more uncertainty or weaker prospects usually mean a lower one.

Banc of California currently trades on a P/E of 19.24x, compared with an industry average of 11.98x and a peer group average of 15.03x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary Fair Ratio for Banc of California is 17.36x. This Fair Ratio is designed to be more tailored than simple peer or industry comparisons, because it incorporates factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, risk profile, industry group and market capitalization.

On this basis, the current P/E of 19.24x sits above the Fair Ratio of 17.36x, which points to the shares looking overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1442 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Banc of California Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. On Simply Wall St’s Community page you can use Narratives, which let you spell out your story for Banc of California, link that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin forecasts, turn those into a Fair Value, see how that Fair Value compares to the current price to guide your buy or sell timing, and then watch your view update automatically as new earnings or news arrive. One investor might build a Narrative around the higher US$22.14 Fair Value with strong digital banking progress and merger execution front of mind, while another might base a lower Fair Value closer to US$15.00 on concerns about commercial real estate exposure, deposit costs and integration risk.

Do you think there's more to the story for Banc of California? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

Apollo Global Management Investment Narrative Recap

To own Apollo, you generally need to believe in its ability to source and structure complex, asset-backed deals that support fee and spread earnings while managing execution and regulatory risks. The xAI data-center financing and the MAPS 2026-1 aviation ABS both fit this playbook, but they do not materially change the near term focus on disciplined growth and internal execution, nor do they remove the risk that conservative growth targets could constrain earnings if markets reward faster expansion.

Among recent announcements, the ongoing share buybacks under Apollo’s US$3.0 billion repurchase program are most relevant here, as they sit alongside new capital-intensive financings like xAI and MAPS 2026-1. Together, they frame a near term catalyst around how effectively Apollo balances returning cash to shareholders with deploying capital into higher yielding, asset-backed opportunities without stretching its internal resources or risk controls.

Yet for all the appeal of high profile AI and aviation deals, investors should also be aware that increased competition in key lending and insurance markets could...

Read the full narrative on Apollo Global Management (it's free!)

Apollo Global Management's narrative projects $1.1 billion revenue and $6.6 billion earnings by 2028. This implies revenues will decline by 64.6% per year, while earnings need to rise by about $3.5 billion from $3.1 billion today.

Uncover how Apollo Global Management's forecasts yield a $158.22 fair value, a 10% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Six fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$110 to US$214.84, underscoring how differently individual investors view Apollo’s worth. Set against this, the risk that internal execution challenges could blunt the benefits of complex deals like the xAI financing gives you a concrete issue to weigh as you compare those perspectives.

data.title: Do Okvau’s Latest Gold Sales Clarify Emerald Resources’ (ASX:EMR) Long-Term Cash Generation Potential?
data.description:
• Emerald Resources NL reported that its 100% owned Okvau Gold Mine in Cambodia produced 25,030 ounces of gold in the December 2025 quarter, with 21,372 ounces poured and 20,410 ounces sold at an average price of US$4,118 per ounce.
  • This update gives investors a clearer view of Okvau’s recent operating throughput and realised pricing, key inputs for assessing project performance and revenue generation.
  • We will now examine how Okvau’s December quarter production and strong realised gold price shape Emerald Resources’ broader investment narrative.

Explore 23 top quantum computing companies leading the revolution in next-gen technology and shaping the future with breakthroughs in quantum algorithms, superconducting qubits, and cutting-edge research.

Advertisement

What Is Emerald Resources' Investment Narrative?

To own Emerald Resources, you have to believe that Okvau can keep running efficiently and that management can convert that into disciplined growth, without overreaching in a single-asset jurisdiction. The December 2025 quarter update slots neatly into that thesis: production of 25,030 ounces and sales of 20,410 ounces at about US$4,118 per ounce broadly align with earlier guidance, so it does not radically shift the near term story, but it does ease the pressure around operational delivery after earlier weather disruptions. The key short term catalysts still sit around any update to FY2026 guidance and progress on the underground expansion, while the biggest risks remain concentration in Cambodia, execution on the mine plan and a valuation that already prices in a lot of optimism after a very large five year total return.

However, investors should also be aware of how much hinges on a single operation in Cambodia. Emerald Resources' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Fourteen fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly A$4.62 to over A$80 per share, underlining how far opinions can stretch. Set against that wide range, Okvau’s December quarter delivery and any future guidance changes could meaningfully sway sentiment about how much risk is acceptable for a business so tied to one producing mine.

data.title: Insider Spends US$459k Buying More Shares In Strive Asset Management
data.description:
Investors who take an interest in Strive Asset Management, LLC (NASDAQ:ASST) should definitely note that the CEO, Manager, Matthew Cole, recently paid US$0.92 per share to buy US$459k worth of the stock. That's a very solid buy in our book, and increased their holding by a noteworthy 12%.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

The Last 12 Months Of Insider Transactions At Strive Asset Management

In fact, the recent purchase by Matthew Cole was the biggest purchase of Strive Asset Management shares made by an insider individual in the last twelve months, according to our records. That implies that an insider found the current price of US$0.97 per share to be enticing. While their view may have changed since the purchase was made, this does at least suggest they have had confidence in the company's future. If someone buys shares at well below current prices, it's a good sign on balance, but keep in mind they may no longer see value. Happily, the Strive Asset Management insiders decided to buy shares at close to current prices.

Strive Asset Management insiders may have bought shares in the last year, but they didn't sell any. Their average price was about US$1.04. This is nice to see since it implies that insiders might see value around current prices. You can see the insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year depicted in the chart below. If you click on the chart, you can see all the individual transactions, including the share price, individual, and the date!

See our latest analysis for Strive Asset Management

Strive Asset Management is not the only stock insiders are buying. So take a peek at this free list of under-the-radar companies with insider buying.

Insider Ownership Of Strive Asset Management

Looking at the total insider shareholdings in a company can help to inform your view of whether they are well aligned with common shareholders. Usually, the higher the insider ownership, the more likely it is that insiders will be incentivised to build the company for the long term. It appears that Strive Asset Management insiders own 4.6% of the company, worth about US$43m. This level of insider ownership is good but just short of being particularly stand-out. It certainly does suggest a reasonable degree of alignment.

So What Do The Strive Asset Management Insider Transactions Indicate?

It's certainly positive to see the recent insider purchase. We also take confidence from the longer term picture of insider transactions. But we don't feel the same about the fact the company is making losses. When combined with notable insider ownership, these factors suggest Strive Asset Management insiders are well aligned, and that they may think the share price is too low. While it's good to be aware of what's going on with the insider's ownership and transactions, we make sure to also consider what risks are facing a stock before making any investment decision. Be aware that Strive Asset Management is showing 2 warning signs in our investment analysis, and 1 of those is concerning...

Of course, you might find a fantastic investment by looking elsewhere. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

data.title: Smurfit Westrock (NYSE:SW) Valuation Revisited As Optimistic Analyst Reports Highlight 2026 Rebound Potential
data.description:
Smurfit Westrock (SW) is back in focus after a series of upbeat analyst reports pointing to a potential containerboard industry rebound in 2026, with earnings improvements and cost savings under close investor watch.

See our latest analysis for Smurfit Westrock.

The recent flurry of analyst reports comes as the share price sits at $43.33, with a 1-month share price return of 14.96% and a year to date share price return of 9.42%, while the 1-year total shareholder return of 13.68% decline contrasts with a 3-year total shareholder return of 15.45%, hinting at improving momentum after a weaker spell.

If Smurfit Westrock has you looking at packaging and industrial demand trends, it could also be a good moment to broaden your search with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With the shares at $43.33, a value score of 3 and a price target gap of around 22%, the key question is simple: Is Smurfit Westrock still undervalued here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 19.4% Undervalued

With Smurfit Westrock last closing at $43.33 against a narrative fair value of $53.73, the valuation hinges on how convincingly future earnings and margin gains materialize.

Ongoing realization of at least $400 million in identified synergies, with management highlighting a similar or greater opportunity from further commercial and operational improvements, should result in sustained increases in margins and operating leverage, materially boosting future earnings.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue growth, margin reset and profit multiple are built into that fair value? The narrative ties all three into one tight earnings story.

Result: Fair Value of $53.73 (UNDERVALUED)

However, this depends on packaging demand remaining stable and loss-making contracts being fixed, as weaker volumes or slower exits could quickly challenge that upside story.

Find out about the key risks to this Smurfit Westrock narrative.

Another View: What The P/E Ratio Is Saying

That 19.4% narrative discount suggests upside, but the P/E ratio paints a tighter picture. Smurfit Westrock trades on 30.2x earnings, compared with 21x for the North American packaging group and a fair ratio of 29.1x. This points to limited room for error if the earnings story hits a bump.
